findings.message: Duplicate data-access layer: both 'repository.py' and 'item_service.py' provide similar data operations (list_items/fetch_all_items, create_item/add_item). This violates separation of concerns and creates maintenance burden. The codebase now has two parallel data abstractions.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate to a single data-access layer. Choose one (repository or item_service) and remove the duplicate. Update all imports consistently across routes, seed.py, and other modules.

findings.message: Route handler directly manages session lifecycle (session.commit(), session.refresh(item)). This business logic should be encapsulated in the data-access layer, not exposed in the route handler.
findings.suggestion: Move session commit/refresh into the data-access layer function. The route handler should only call add_item() and return the result. Consider using repository.create_item() which already handles this, or modify item_service.add_item() to match that behavior.
